task_completion.description: Approve the legitimate comments from David Chen, Sarah Mitchell, and Maria Rodriguez.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the WordPress Comments page. Verify that comments by David Chen, Sarah Mitchell, and Maria Rodriguez are present and approved. They should appear in the 'Approved' tab, or if in the 'All' tab, they should not have the yellow 'pending' background and should show an 'Unapprove' action link instead of 'Approve'.

task_completion.description: Mark the spam comments from BestPricesMeds, SEOExpert2024, CryptoKing99, and FreeGiftCards as spam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the WordPress Comments page. Verify that comments by BestPricesMeds, SEOExpert2024, CryptoKing99, and FreeGiftCards are no longer visible in the 'Pending' or 'Approved' lists. They should either be visible in the 'Spam' tab or completely removed from the main view.
Look for an indented reply comment directly underneath Sarah Mitchell's original comment in the WordPress admin panel, indicating the agent successfully submitted a response.

task_completion.description: Include the keyword 'Yoast' in the reply to Sarah Mitchell.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the text of the submitted reply to Sarah Mitchell. Verify that the text contains the word 'Yoast'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the pending moderation queue is completely clear.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the top filter links on the Comments page (All | Pending | Approved | Spam | Trash) or the left sidebar menu. Verify the 'Pending' count is exactly 0, or the 'Pending' tab displays a message indicating no comments are awaiting moderation.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress web interface in a browser to moderate comments.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the actual WordPress wp-admin interface, not a terminal using WP-CLI or database commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the output in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The replies and comment statuses must be shown within the genuine WordPress admin dashboard, not typed into a notepad, text editor, or a locally created HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress reply functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking 'Reply' under the comment and typing into the WordPress inline reply text box.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Legitimate authors to approve: David Chen, Sarah Mitchell, Maria Rodriguez. Spam authors to mark as spam: BestPricesMeds, SEOExpert2024, CryptoKing99, FreeGiftCards. Reply keyword required for Sarah Mitchell's comment: 'Yoast'. Expected final pending comment count: 0.
